identity.lead_description: Single female influencer remake subject driven by the default influencer references, preserved as the same recognizable person before and after the outfit change.

wardrobe.change_policy: Treat the casual look and the cosplay look as separate scene-bound outfits connected only by the dedicated hand-wipe transition.
locked_relationships: scene_001 uses a casual pink tee and gray sweatpants baseline look
scene_003 uses a Finn-inspired cosplay silhouette that should visibly match the preview card shown in scene_001
scene_002 is a transition wipe and should not introduce a third wardrobe concept

room_signature: black ceiling fan at the top edge
small shelf on the left wall
desk and hanging vine decor on the right
background.change_policy: Do not move to a new room, change lighting dramatically, or redesign the bedroom.

composition.framing: centered upper-thigh or full-body framing with the performer facing camera
composition.look: clean social-video realism with space reserved for the scene_001 overlay card

elements: pale pink fitted baby tee with cute cartoon-cat chest graphic
long straight hair with small side clips
white bear-ear hat with ear flaps
cobalt blue mini-skirt or lower hem

text.pattern: No intentional text. Source TikTok watermark and username drift between lower-left and lower-right positions and should be removed rather than recreated.

global: small playful hand choreography and light torso sway
no walking away from camera and no camera travel

scene_overrides.motion_intent: light TikTok-style hand shapes near the face with small hip and shoulder sway
fast practical hand-over-lens wipe that creates a full-frame skin-toned blur
playful cosplay sway with wrist flicks, shoulder bops, and slight backpack bounce

overlay_layer_requirements.description: Square lower-center reference card showing a blonde cartoon adventurer in a white bear-ear hat, turquoise outfit, and lime-green backpack
overlay_layer_requirements.compositing_rule: Keep it as a separate image overlay for the whole scene instead of baking it into the generated base clip.

notes: Nano Banana 2 is the default startframe model because both main looks depend on multi-reference composition of the locked lead identity plus the source-scene framing.
Grok Imagine remains the outfit-edit fallback if a later workflow preserves an approved base frame and only swaps the wardrobe.
Kling V3 is sufficient for the dance beats and the short hand-wipe transition because no explicit motion-transfer clip is required.
